topics: 🐂 Four avot of damages: שור, בור, מבעה, הבער
📖 Yerushalmi identifies שור as קרן from the pesukim
🌾 מבעה includes רגל and שן through field-damage verses
🧩 Why the Mishnah counts four while R' Chiyya counts thirteen

overview.title: 📖 Overview — The Opening Framework of Nezikin
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The Mishnah opens with four primary categories of damage. The Yerushalmi immediately asks what each av means in the pesukim, then expands the conversation: if there are more liability categories in other beraitot, why does our Mishnah choose exactly four?
overview.hebrewSource: אַרְבָּעָה אָבוֹת נְזִיקִין: הַשּׁוֹר וְהַבּוֹר וְהַמַּבְעֶה וְהַהֶבְעֵר

slides.title: 🧱 The Four Avot
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah gives the master list before the Yerushalmi defines the details.

boxes.title: 🐂 השור
items: Source: כי יגוף שור איש את שור רעהו
Starts with tam, then asks for mu'ad

boxes.title: 🕳️ הבור
items: A hazard created in public space

boxes.title: 🌾 המבעה
items: Yerushalmi connects it to רגל and שן

callout.label: Key move:
callout.text: The Mishnah is not just listing cases. It is building a liability taxonomy.

slides.title: 🔎 How the Yerushalmi Defines Each Av
slides.subtitle: The sugya anchors the categories in pesukim.

columns.label: Tam
columns.content: כי יגוף שור איש את שור רעהו

columns.label: Mu'ad
columns.content: או נודע כי שור נגח הוא

nodes.content: The av is defined through the Torah's damage language.

callout.label: Yerushalmi style:
callout.text: Short, dense, and source-driven. The page moves quickly from Mishnah category to pasuk anchor.

slides.title: 💬 Four or Thirteen?
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi compares our Mishnah with R' Chiyya's larger list.

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: Our Mishnah taught: אַרְבָּעָה אָבוֹת נְזִיקִין — four primary categories.

lines.speaker: RḤ
lines.text: But R' Chiyya taught thirteen: damages, pain, medical costs, loss of work, shame, and the watchmen categories.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: Answer: our Mishnah is about property-damage frameworks, while R' Chiyya includes bodily injury and custodial liabilities too.

callout.label: Practical reading:
callout.text: The number depends on what universe you are counting. The Mishnah narrows to core damage categories. R' Chiyya broadens to liability categories.

slides.title: 📊 Avot and Toldot on the Daf
slides.subtitle: The sugya starts mapping derivatives under each primary category.
rows: נגיחה, נגיפה, נשיכה, רביצה, בעיטה, דחייה
Obstacles in public domain, like a coal causing a stumble
Liability for a hazard one creates
Animal enters private property and damages by walking or load
Eating benefit: barley, oil, edible items
Damage where the animal derives benefit

callout.label: Important:
callout.text: This follows the Yerushalmi text pulled from Sefaria, especially segments 4–7 of Jerusalem Talmud Bava Kamma 1:1.

slides.title: 🧠 The Mishnah's Logic
slides.subtitle: Why not learn all damage categories from one example?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Each av has a feature the others do not fully share.
2️⃣ Living/moving damage is unlike a stationary pit.
3️⃣ A pit is unlike fire, which spreads differently.
4️⃣ The shared denominator is: דרכן להזיק ושמירתן עליך.
5️⃣ Therefore: when they damage, the mazikin pays במיטב הארץ.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The daf teaches how Nezikin starts: define the category, find its Torah source, then test what it can and cannot teach.

summary.principle: The opening daf builds the basic map of liability: four avot, their sources, their derivatives, and the reason each category must be guarded.

flowSteps.content: Yerushalmi anchors each category in pesukim and then asks how broad the count should be.

🎓 Key Principle:
Liability begins when the object or force normally causes damage and guarding it is your responsibility.

quiz.q: What are the four avot listed in the opening Mishnah?
quiz.e: The Mishnah opens with השור, הבור, המבעה, וההבער.

quiz.q: How does the Yerushalmi initially define 'השור'?
quiz.e: The Yerushalmi says: השור זה הקרן, and brings verses about a goring ox.

quiz.q: Why does R' Chiyya's list have thirteen instead of four?
o: He includes broader liability categories, including bodily injury and custodians
quiz.e: The Yerushalmi distinguishes the Mishnah's narrower damage framework from R' Chiyya's broader count.

quiz.q: Which phrase expresses the shared denominator of the avot?
quiz.e: The Mishnah says their way is to damage and guarding them is your responsibility.

quiz.q: What is the main technical issue for a Yerushalmi version of the Bavli site?
o: The Yerushalmi API is structured by chapter/halakhah/segment, so daf/page mapping must be handled separately
Quiz pages cannot work on GitHub Pages
Hebrew text cannot render in HTML
quiz.e: Unlike the Bavli pipeline, Sefaria's Yerushalmi refs are not simple daf/amud pages. We need mapping from page markers or chapter/halakhah units.
